'''Danny Houghton''' (born 25 September 1988) is a former English [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. Born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], he has played his entire career for his hometown club, and had recently come back to the game after retirement to play for Leigh miners, and has represented the [[England Knights]] internationally.

==Background==
Houghton was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], Humberside, England.

==Early career==
Signing for Hull from amateur side [[East Hull A.R.L.F.C.|East Hull]], he progressed through the club's academy ranks to make his début in 2007. His position of choice is {{rlp|hk}}, although he only established himself there during his time in the Hull academy, having previously played as a [[Rugby league positions#Scrum-half|scrum-half]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

==Playing career==
===Hull F.C.===
The start of the 2008 season saw Houghton gain a regular place in the first team, often being used from the bench.
[[File:Danny Howts.jpg|thumb|right|upright=1.2|Houghton in action for Hull in 2008]]
Houghton quickly established himself as Hull's starting {{rlp|hk}}, and was also handed the vice-captaincy of the team. He was in Hull's [[2013 Challenge Cup]] Final team, losing out to [[Wigan Warriors]] by a score of 16–0.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10264382/Challenge-Cup-final-2013-Hull-FC-0-Wigan-Warriors-16.html | title=Challenge Cup final 2013: Hull FC 0 Wigan Warriors 16 | date=24 August 2013 | newspaper=The Telegraph | location=UK | first=Arindam | last=Rej | access-date=14 February 2020 }}</ref>

Houghton had his most successful season to date in 2016. He starred in Hull's [[Challenge Cup]]-winning team, making a try-saving tackle in the final minutes as Hull beat [[Warrington Wolves]] 12–10.<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12–10 Warrington Wolves|work=BBC Sport|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

As a result of Houghton's incredible season he was awarded with the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] award for [[Super League]] player of the year, the first Hull F.C. player to win the award.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/oct/03/danny-houghton-hull-fc-man-of-steel|title=Danny Houghton makes Hull FC history with Man of Steel award|date=3 October 2016|newspaper=The Guardian|access-date=20 November 2020}}</ref> He also won the [[Man of Steel Awards#Super League Hit Man|Super League Hit Man]] award for most tackles in a season with 1289 tackles, an award that he had previously won on three occasions, and was included in the 2016 [[Super League Dream Team]].

Houghton's success would continue in 2017 when Hull F.C. won the Challenge Cup for a second year in a row with an 18–14 win over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18–14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref> a repeat of the 2013 final that he had also played in.

In January 2018, Houghton was appointed as the Hull F.C. captain.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/news/2018-01-24-houghton-backing-big-crowd-for-season-opener|title=Houghton Backing Big Crowd for Season Opener!|publisher=Hull F.C.|date=24 January 2018|access-date=28 January 2018|archive-date=28 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180128074754/http://www.hullfc.com/news/2018-01-24-houghton-backing-big-crowd-for-season-opener|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played 14 games for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 2020 Super League season including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] as they got to within one game of the grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|work=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=20 November 2020}}</ref>

In April 2021, Houghton broke the rugby league record for the most tackles in a single match. He made 85 tackles against [[Warrington Wolves]],<ref>{{cite web|last=Jackson|first=William|date=19 April 2021|title=The astonishing stats from FC's Warrington stalemate as Houghton smashes record|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-houghton-super-league-record-5314669|access-date=20 April 2021|website=HullLive}}</ref> surpassing the previous record held by [[Elijah Taylor (rugby league)|Elijah Taylor]], who made 77 tackles for [[Wests Tigers]] in a match against [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in 2015.
Houghton played 22 games for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>
On 30 August 2024, Houghton announced that he would be retiring from rugby league at the end of the 2024 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c75newdg535o|title=Hull FC legend Houghton to retire at end of season|website=www.bbc.co.uk|access-date=31 August 2024}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Houghton represented the [[England Knights]], making his debut against [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 2011.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Career of Danny Houghton |url=https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2020/07/13/the-career-of-danny-houghton/ |publisher=Hull F.C. |access-date=22 June 2023 |date=13 July 2020}}</ref> He was selected as the Knights' captain for the [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|2012 European Cup]] tournament.<ref>{{cite web |title=Houghton to captain England Knights |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/houghton-to-captain-england-knights |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=22 June 2023 |date=10 October 2012}}</ref>

He is eligible to represent [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], but turned down the opportunity to play for them, as he hoped to be selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Hull FC's Danny Houghton holding out for England cap |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/10784956/hull-fcs-danny-houghton-holding-out-for-england-cap |website=Sky Sports |access-date=22 June 2023 |date=28 February 2017}}</ref> He was called up by England for the first time in 2017 for a training camp as part of the team's preparations for the upcoming [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Houghton hoping to fulfil his dreams via England camps |url=https://www.totalrl.com/houghton-hoping-fulfil-dreams-via-england-camps/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=22 June 2023 |date=1 March 2017}}</ref> but is still uncapped.
